name: arch-test
title: arch-test
summary: detect architectures supported by your machine/kernel
description:
detect architectures supported by your machine/kernel
This package lets you enumerate architectures that your kernel can run.
The check is for the ability to run machine code and supporting appropriate
syscall ABI -- you may need to install userland libraries in a chroot,
container or via multiarch to actually execute non-static binaries of such
architectures.
.
Also provided is "elf-arch", a tool to determine the architecture of an ELF
binary (program or library).
.
Architectures detected by this version of arch-test are:
* amd64, i386, x32
* arm, armel, armhf, arm64, arm64ilp32
* powerpc, ppc64, ppc64el, powerpcspe
* m68k
* sh4
* s390x
* sparc, sparc64
* illumos-amd64
* win32, win64
* kfreebsd-amd64, kfreebsd-i386
* alpha
* hppa
* riscv64, riscv32
* arc
* loong64

commands.name: arch-test
commands.description: detect architectures your kernel can run binaries of

arguments.description: detect architectures your kernel can run binaries of
arguments.raw: -test  detect architectures your kernel can run binaries of

commands.name: elf-arch
commands.description: recognize architecture of an ELF executable/shlib

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use arch-test because detect architectures supported by your machine/kernel.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use arch-test as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arch-test [-n]` as documented upstream for arch-test.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `arch-test [-n] [-c <chroot>] <arch>` as documented upstream for arch-test.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair arch-test with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `arch-test --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -test
arguments.description: detect architectures your kernel can run binaries of

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: detect architectures supported by your machine/kernel.
